text: Trump: Iran was a great country if you go back 25 years ago or so.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇱🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump stated that Iran should accept a ceasefire otherwise the consequences are too great. "Well, they should do it. But, again, the consequences are great. I mean, they were told you if you protest, you will be shot immediately.…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump on Iranians asking for the continuation of the U.S. air campaign: "We've had numerous intercepts where people are saying, 'Please keep bombing.' Bombs are dropping near their homes, 'Please keep bombing. Do it.' And these are people who are living where the bombs are exploding. And when we leave and we're not hitting those areas, they're saying, 'Please come back. Come back. Come back.' These are the people—I don't know what they do. All I can tell you is they want freedom. It's a violent, horrible world where if you protest, you get shot." @wfwitness
Trump: The Iranians are saying, “Please keep bombing.” These people live where the bombs are exploding. We leave, and they say, “Please come back, come back, come back.”
Reporter: Why would the Iranians want you to blow up their infrastructure? Trump: They would be willing to suffer that in order to have freedom.
Trump: Every single thing has been thought out by all of us, but I can’t reveal the plan to the media.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇱🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump on Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Cain: "Perfecto. You know, I got to know general Cain in my first term because he was able to take out ISIS in four weeks instead of the four year projection." @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump stated that Iran should accept a ceasefire otherwise the consequences are too great. "Well, they should do it. But, again, the consequences are great. I mean, they were told you if you protest, you will be shot immediately. Saw what happened to the young wrestler, he was a great champion. " @wfwitness
Reporter: What would be your reaction if the Iranians rose up against the regime during a ceasefire? Trump: They should do it, but the consequences are great.

WarMonitor Trump didn’t want us to know, so what else are they hiding? And now threatening reporters with jail… that’s straight out of a dictator’s playbook. Trump: "They didn't know there was somebody missing until this leaker gave the information. Whoever it was, we think we'll be able to find out, because we're gonna go to the media company that released it and we're gonna say, 'National security. Give it up or go to jail.'" https://twitter.com/atrupar/status/2041205951571587443/video/1 - Aaron Rupar tweet

Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 U.S. Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Cain technical specifications needed to rescue the missing crew member from Iranian soil: A U.S. Central Command plan was approved by the secretary and the president, and shortly thereafter, a U.S. Air Force combat search and rescue task force comprised of 10 Warthogs in their sandy role, HC-130 Combat King IIs, HH-60 Jolly Green II helicopters, and Air Force special warfare airmen—a package comprised of combat rescue officers and pararescuemen operators—audaciously penetrated behind enemy lines. tweet
Trump: Hegseth was treated very unfairly. People who treated him unfairly are now calling me and saying, “What a choice.”

Open Source Intel Trump: We needed a lot more equipment going in than coming out, because we needed to be able to scale mountains. But the sand was -- it was sandy, wet sand. We thought there may be a problem taking off because of the weight of the plane… We blew them up. We didn’t want anyone examining our anti-aircraft and other equipment. They were old planes. tweet

Faytuks News RT @JenniferJJacobs : News: The pilot of the F-15 downed in Iran had serious injuries. There was a time shortly after he ejected when Trump officials were worried he would not make it. The very early reports from the battlefield as he was being transported sounded dire. He is OK now, I'm told. The weapons systems officer who was rescued later was banged up and dehydrated, but his injuries weren't as serious as the pilot, sources told @CBSNews tweet

Open Source Intel U.S. Secretary of Defense/War Pete Hegseth: "Today will be the largest volume of strikes (on Iran) since day one. Tomorrow? Even more than today." Contributed by @AZ_Intel_ . https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2041210366173069413/video/1 tweet

Open Source Intel Trump: "A lighter, faster aircraft came in and we blew up the old planes. We blew them up to smithereens. Because we had equipment on the planes that frankly we'd like to take, but I don't think it was worthwhile. We have the best equipment anywhere in the world. We didn't want anybody examining our anti-aircraft and other equipment." tweet

Faytuks News New information on this incident via Reuters: The two Qatari LNG tankers had been allowed by Iran to transit the Strait of Hormuz However, while the ships were in transit the IRGC suddenly ordered them to hold without explanation A pair of LNG tankers aiming to make a run at the Strait of Hormuz this morning have reversed course and turned around. The two vessels are carrying Qatari LNG loaded in February, with one indicating that it was headed for China. AIS via @MarineTraffic. https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2041074746087952465/photo/1 - OSINTtechnical tweet

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇬🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 The secretive American RQ-180 stealth reconnaissance drone was spotted for the second time yesterday making a landing in Larissa, Greece. This is our best view to date of the highly classified and yet to be aknowledged aircraft. RQ-180s are believed to be deployed at the Greek airfield to support operation in Iran. It could also be used to conduct high altitude reconnaissance over the Black Sea and Ukraine. Credit: Efthymios Siakaras See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

And he was injured, and he did an amazing, amazing thing. He scaled cliff faces, bleeding rather profusely. @wfwitness
